basically a Labradoodle with a lot of Cocker Spaniels.

well, now it's… now it's me.

marketing activities across our big portfolio.

that's a… it's an organic driver.

end of last year, and, you know, now it's, like, working to really kind of lean into the owned channel.

Like, we learned from Google.

now I think we have more visibility into the fact that, like, a keyword strategy works, like, what the, you know, what the posting cadence should be, what the content should be.
I know you have a pricing piece as well, which I'm interested to learn about with the transparency, also.
You mentioned that on… in your email, so I was curious… you're… curious where you were… were at with that.
Yeah, we're, so Massachusetts rolled out on September 2nd, so… We are, you know, we have fees listed on our website for every single property.
We activated rent calculators for Massachusetts, we have 12 properties here.
Colorado's the next state to go.

We're kind of waiting… we have calculator functionality available, that we, you know, developed into our website, but, we're relying on There's no data source, like, there's no… the PMSs can't support the data feed for the fees yet, they're working on it.
So until Yardi kind of gets there, we probably won't roll out unless we have, you know, regulations telling us that we… guiding us to.
The regulations don't say you need a calculator, but it just kind of goes along with that best practice of that, like, total monthly leasing price.
So, we're hoping Yardi gets, you know.
they're close, we're hoping they get closer by the end of the year, and we can just deploy across the portfolio, because it's the right thing to do for the customer.
But what that means from, like, a… Pricing perspective in terms of, like, schema and, you know.

putting rents on GBP profiles and, in her paid ads, like, it's just… there's more complexity there that we have to kind of work through to make sure that we remain compliant when these regulations start to become more… Common.

Now and Yardi?

super important for Google, because we're displaying pricing, so we can display total monthly leasing price, and then the, non… what's the word I'm looking for?

base rent.
